textResponse:
I'll search for the latest AI agent infrastructure announcements this week.Based on the latest news, here are the top AI agent infrastructure announcements this week:

## Key Announcements

**Microsoft's Agent Governance Toolkit**
Microsoft released the Agent Governance Toolkit, an open-source security shield for AI agents protecting against 10 critical attack types—goal hijacking, memory poisoning, and rogue agents. The toolkit works instantly (under 0.1 milliseconds) to block dangerous agent actions before they execute.

**NeuBird AI's Falcon Agent**
NeuBird AI announced Falcon, a next-generation version of their agent that is easily three times faster than their previous version and is averaging around 92% in confidence scores. The company raised $19.3 million in funding, bringing NeuBird AI's total funding to approximately $64 million.

**MCP Becomes Industry Standard**
Every major AI provider now ships MCP-compatible tooling, and the protocol has become the default mechanism by which agents connect to external tools, APIs, and data sources. The Linux Foundation also announced it would take Anthropic's MCP under open governance, further cementing its status as industry-wide shared infrastructure.

**Microsoft and MYOB Partnership**
Microsoft and MYOB announced a five-year partnership to bring AI agents directly into small business tools. MYOB will use Microsoft's Agent 365 for governance and deploy customer-facing agents that forecast cash flow and guide compliance.

**Payment Industry Expansions**
Visa has deployed advanced artificial intelligence to help solve payment disputes and expedite corporate bill pay; while Mastercard adds Hong Kong to its global agentic commerce network.
